reviews.review_description:
The graphics of the textures are basic but still fine. Unfortunately, if you want to play with others, you need more headsets in the same room, i think. So otherwise, you can only play basketball and darts.



 Thank you for the reply. Yes, it's true, I forgot the billiards and the Rubik's cube, the decorations were not included in the review. But what about the other games? How can I play with someone in multiplayer in mixed reality? Because I have tried that too but without success. Note that I do not speak English as a native speaker, so perhaps I missed something due to misunderstanding.

developer_response.body:
Current offerings include AIR HOCKEY (With AI trained with bespoke Reinforcement Learning), BASKETBALL, DARTS, BILLIARDS/POOL, TABLE TENNIS, FOOSBALL, PUZZLE CUBE, AND DECORATIVE OBJECTS. Everything in the promo videos as well as the store descriptions. And they are all available in colocated mode with many more to come and with constant upgrades!

----------------------

Hi, thanks so much for your review! We really appreciate your feedback. Just to clarify for others reading, all of the items as advertised are available in both single-player and multiplayer, not just basketball and darts. Current offerings include basketball, darts, pool, table tennis, air hockey, foosball, puzzle cube, and many decorative objects.

Some of the more competitive items like foosball and air hockey were designed with multiple players in mind through our colocated mode, but they can still be spawned and explored in single-player. Looking ahead, we’re exploring AI opponents for these games to make them more engaging for solo players, though that’s still in development.

In the meantime, if you get the chance, we’d love for you to try out the colocated experience; it’s something unique in today’s XR market. Multiplayer setup is simple: one player taps the Server button (upper left corner of the menu while in the single player mode), others join via the Client button (on the same Wi-Fi). Then just align the blue and yellow orbs in the same physical spot, and you’re synced. Thanks again for taking the time to share your thoughts!

- Jay

reviews.review_description: Fun mixed reality game with moderate exercise.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words! We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the moderate exercise. Creating fun ways to stay active is one of our favorite parts of developing AnyRec.

With the Mega Update coming June 9th, we're expanding the number of interactive, networked activities like basketball, billiards, and foosball to make movement-based gameplay even more engaging, whether you're playing solo or with friends.

Thanks again for being part of the AnyRec community. Your support means the world to us!

reviews.review_description: Found this on the app lab and I have to say this exceeded my expectations for a mixed reality game. The physics on the basketball and pool are flawless and everything gets saved exactly where I placed them in my room. The game does lack a bit of models for room decor, but seeing as how this is early access I find this to be a steal for two dollars. My kids and I are having a blast. I honestly can’t find a comparable game anywhere on the store. Cheers!

developer_response.body: We are ecstatic to hear that you are enjoying our game! As you pointed out, we are in the process of generating more assets for room decor along with many other features. We hope that you will continue enjoying our game.

reviews.review_description: The simplicity paired with the diverse content in the Anyrec game transforms my daily monotony into joy. The consistent updates they offer are truly touching. I'm grateful to you for this.

currently it's a bit limited and I don't know whether to keep it or not depends on what kind of development it does in the future.

for example, it doesn't take real furniture into account, so objects like a ball don't bounce off a sofa but pass through it.

there are no shadows although I admit they are a bit difficult for the standalone quest, but with the quest3 it would be possible.

any virtual furniture is very difficult to apply because it needs to be positioned with great precision and once positioned it only takes a moment and you accidentally reposition it.
and this is very frustrating.

virtual furniture does not take into account collisions with walls or collisions with the floor.

a possible future development would be to be able to have a vast quantity of even unusual objects that you can put in your room that replace real objects and real furniture.

for example, although it would be nice to have virtual beds to apply on the real bed, it would be equally nice to have objects of the same size but completely different.

objects must have physics and weight, many objects have no gravity.

there must be collisions on objects and hand tracking with collisions and hand physics, like the handpsytic lab

at that point the app would be interesting

Oh, and the mechanism for zooming in and out on objects currently doesn't work

the current value is three and a half stars, I don't feel like giving three stars so I give 4 stars.
hoping that there will be improvements soon so that I can keep it and not return it

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your purchase and your concerns are heard. We wanted to make the game as dynamic as possible without overcomplicating the mechanics. We agree that players should be allowed to scale objects freely, and we are actively deciding what would be the most natural and user friendly way to do so. 

In regards to balls falling through sofa, we made sure to add collisions to furniture that have been set up through Meta's play space setup. If the collision still does not work after the play space setup, please leave us a comment through the feedback system. We will appreciate it.

As for many of the objects not having gravity or collision detection, we wanted to balance playability with performance. While we can optimize performance of the colliders even further and these solutions are ready to be deployed, simply having them on by default will make most users experience a performance hit. Going back to not overcomplicating the mechanics, this would increase the need for more toggle-able options. However, this is one of our top priorities as we agree that it would be nice to have options to turn on collision for static objects.

Thank you again for your purchase, and we hope that you get to see our continuing evolution.
